claim: ruff S608 / bandit B608: possible SQL injection in models.py (lines 54, 69, 127, 135).
rationale: All four f-string SQL statements interpolate ONLY internally-built fragments, never user input. Line 54/69 wrap `query` from _build_contact_query (constant column list + parameterized `?` placeholders); ORDER BY (line ~83) uses order_col from the allowed_sorts whitelist dict and a DESC/ASC ternary; lines 127/135 interpolate id_filter which is a literal 'AND id != ?' or ''. Every user value (search/name/phone/contact_type/letter/sort) flows through `?` params or a whitelist. No injection. Confirmed by two independent reviewers.

claim: ruff S603 / subprocess call with untrusted input in routes/sync.py:51.
rationale: subprocess.run([sys.executable, auth_script], ...) uses the list form (no shell=True, no shell interpretation) and both args are fully controlled: sys.executable is the interpreter path and auth_script is built from os.path.dirname(__file__), not user input. No injection vector. S603 fires on every subprocess call regardless of safety.

The match is a `dedup_key` hash emitted by the audit tool into its own report JSON, not a credential. The file is an auto-generated audit artifact and is .gitignored (docs/AUTOMATED_AUDIT_REPORT* excluded), so it is not even in the published repo. No secret.

claim: ruff S101: use of assert detected (~136 hits, all in tests/).
rationale: All S101 hits are pytest assertions in tests/test_models.py and tests/test_routes.py. assert is the correct and intended idiom in a pytest suite; S101 only matters for production code that runs under python -O. Not applicable to test files.

claim: routes/sync.py:49 auth-script path resolves one dir too high so the auth button is broken.
rationale: FALSE. __file__ is routes/sync.py (one level deep), so os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(__file__)) resolves to the PROJECT ROOT, exactly where google_auth.py lives alongside google_sync.py. Verified: dirname(routes/sync.py)=routes/, dirname(routes/)=project root. The double-dirname is correct because sync.py is nested in routes/. Auth flow is not broken.

claim: routes/contacts.py passes sort/dir query params unvalidated into list_contacts (SQL injection sink).
rationale: The route passes sort/dir raw, but models.list_contacts whitelists them: order_col = allowed_sorts.get(sort, 'name COLLATE NOCASE') maps to a fixed set of column expressions, and direction = 'DESC' if sort_dir=='desc' else 'ASC'. An injection payload like ?sort=name);DROP falls through to the default. No unsanitized value reaches the ORDER BY string. Validation lives in the model, not the route, but it IS validated.
